---
title: "Cyclic Memory Residue Winding"
---

A cyclic memory slot is a residue. A memory event also has a winding number:
how many full turns through the memory bank have happened before this event.

::: {.lesson-goal}

## Goal

Use residue plus winding to explain aliasing in a finite memory bank without
pretending the residue alone proves freshness.

:::

## Concept

The circular object is the memory bank `C_bank_size`. Event `t` writes to
slot `t mod bank_size`, while `t div bank_size` records the winding. Two events
can share a slot and still be different writes because their windings differ.

```text
event index = winding * bank_size + residue slot
```

That is the whole point of the contract: keep alias provenance explicit.

## Example

The public fixture uses `bank_size = 8`, `event_index = 23`, and
`event_count = 32`. The event sits in residue slot `7` with winding `2`.
The same residue class inside the trace is:

```text
events:   7, 15, 23, 31
windings: 0,  1,  2,  3
```

```bash
python scripts/cyclic_memory_certify.py --format json
python scripts/circle_ai_contract_ready.py --kind cyclic_memory_residue_winding --digest --field same_residue_events --field same_residue_windings --field max_alias_load
```

## Theorem Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIM-T0005"></div>

## Certificate Reading

The useful fields are:

- `residue_slot`: the finite memory address.
- `winding`: the full-turn count for the selected event.
- `same_residue_events`: every event in the trace that aliases the same slot.
- `same_residue_windings`: the corresponding provenance layer for those aliases.
- `max_alias_load`: the largest finite alias class size in the declared trace.

Planner recommendations:

- `MEMORY-ATTACH-WINDING-ALIAS-PROVENANCE` says a downstream system should preserve winding when slots alias.
- `MEMORY-AUDIT-FINITE-ALIAS-LOAD` says the finite trace should expose the maximum observed alias load rather than hiding it behind a slot id.

::: {.warning-box}
This is a finite alias-provenance contract. It does not prove cache freshness,
retrieval quality, long-term memory quality, or safety of any production memory
system. Python fixtures are executable references, not proof artifacts and not
performance claims.
:::

## Dictionary

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0028"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0081"></div>

## Source Trail

<p data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY.md">Attention and memory paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_AI_02_COIL_ATTENTION_AND_MEMORY.md">Coil Attention And Memory</a></p>

<p>Quickstart: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/docs/CYCLIC_MEMORY_CERTIFIER_QUICKSTART.md">Cyclic Memory Certifier</a></p>

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="strided_candidate_fanout.qmd">Previous: Strided Candidate Fanout</a>
<a href="multicoil_phase_feature.qmd">Next: Multicoil Phase Features</a>
</div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
